description: 55M with diabetes, HTN, LDL 145, ASCVD risk 9.2%. Diabetes triggers statin per ACC/AHA Section 4.3. Risk ≥7.5% supports high-intensity consideration.

actions.label: Initiate statin therapy (moderate- or high-intensity)
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: adults aged 40-75 with diabetes should receive at least moderate-intensity statin. With 10-year ASCVD risk of 9.2% (≥7.5%), high-intensity statin to achieve ≥50% LDL-C reduction is reasonable (COR IIa). Patient is statin-naive.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 142/88 on amlodipine exceeds target <130/80 for patient with diabetes. HTN is an independent ASCVD risk factor. Consider adding or titrating antihypertensive.

actions.label: Continue and optimize diabetes management
actions.rationale: Patient is on metformin. Glycemic control status not fully characterized in context, but ongoing diabetes management supports ASCVD risk reduction.

contraindications.label: Defer statin in favor of lifestyle modifications alone
contraindications.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I recommends statin therapy for all adults 40-75 with diabetes regardless of risk score. Lifestyle alone is insufficient.
